title: Apple’s privacy policy reveals an AI shopping assistant is coming to the Apple Store app
excerpt: Apple is preparing to add an AI-powered shopping assistant to the Apple Store app, according to a privacy policy update spotted by MacRumors. The updated document describes a “Virtual Shopping Assistant” that collects account information, device identifiers, carrier information, chat data, and optionally location data to personalise responses. The feature is not live yet. Apple […]
content_html:
TL;DR

Apple’s updated privacy policy for the Store app reveals a coming “Virtual Shopping Assistant.” It collects chat, device, and location data. Not live yet. Partial opt-out only.

Apple is preparing to add an AI-powered shopping assistant to the Apple Store app, according to a privacy policy update spotted by MacRumors. The updated document describes a “Virtual Shopping Assistant” that collects account information, device identifiers, carrier information, chat data, and optionally location data to personalise responses. The feature is not live yet.

Apple says it removes personal identifiers from chats before sharing data with partners, and that the information is used only to provide better conversational responses. Users cannot fully opt out of the assistant, but they can toggle off a “Chat Improvements” setting that controls whether Apple uses their conversations to improve the system. The setting is found under Account > Settings in the Apple Store app.

The assistant would be Apple’s second AI chatbot deployment after a similar feature in the Apple Support app, which is also not available to all users yet. Apple built a third-party AI system for Siri but did not show it at WWDC, and the company has been slower than Google, Amazon, and Meta in deploying conversational AI to consumer-facing products. A shopping assistant in the Store app is a low-risk entry point: the conversation is about products Apple already sells, the data is first-party, and the stakes of a wrong answer are a mismatched iPhone case rather than a medical misdiagnosis.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The privacy language is worth noting. “Where enabled, location data” and sharing with unnamed “partners” after removing identifiers is standard for Apple but more permissive than users might expect from a company that markets itself on privacy. A German court ruled Google liable for the content of its AI-generated responses, and any AI shopping assistant that recommends the wrong product or misquotes a spec could face similar scrutiny. Apple has not announced a launch date.

content_html_zh:
TL;DR

苹果更新的商店应用程序隐私政策揭示了即将推出的“虚拟购物助手”。 它收集聊天、设备和位置数据。 还没活呢。 仅部分选择退出。

根据 MacRumors 发现的隐私政策更新，苹果正准备在 Apple Store 应用程序中添加人工智能购物助手。 更新后的文档描述了一个“虚拟购物助手”，它收集帐户信息、设备标识符、运营商信息、聊天数据以及可选的位置数据以个性化响应。 该功能尚未上线。

苹果表示，在与合作伙伴共享数据之前，它会从聊天中删除个人标识符，并且这些信息仅用于提供更好的对话响应。 用户无法完全选择退出助手，但他们可以关闭“聊天改进”设置，该设置控制苹果是否使用他们的对话来改进系统。 该设置位于 Apple Store 应用中的“帐户”>“设置”下。

该助手将是继苹果支持应用程序中的类似功能之后苹果部署的第二个人工智能聊天机器人，该应用程序也尚未向所有用户提供。 苹果为 Siri 构建了第三方人工智能系统，但没有在 WWDC 上展示，而且该公司在将对话式人工智能部署到面向消费者的产品方面比谷歌、亚马逊和 Meta 慢。 Store应用程序中的购物助手是一个低风险的切入点：对话是关于苹果已经销售的产品，数据是第一方的，错误答案的风险是iPhone手机壳不匹配，而不是医疗误诊。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

隐私语言值得注意。 “启用后，位置数据”以及在删除标识符后与未透露姓名的“合作伙伴”共享是苹果的标准做法，但比用户对一家以隐私为卖点的公司所期望的更为宽松。 德国一家法院裁定谷歌对其人工智能生成的回复内容负责，任何推荐错误产品或错误引用规格的人工智能购物助手都可能面临类似的审查。 苹果尚未宣布发布日期。
